answer:
\boxed{B}

The correct answer is **B. Aragon**, as Ferdinand II was King of Aragon when he married Isabella I of Castile in 1469, uniting their kingdoms to form the basis of modern Spain. This union is famously known as the "Catholic Monarchs" (*Los Reyes Católicos*). Navarre and León were separate kingdoms at the time, while Galicia was a region within Castile.
